objective: Turn the licensed generic VRM development sample into a clearly colored, modest, cute anime-style Hina presentation that is immediately usable in the local desktop: restore its embedded textures without widening renderer network access, apply an original pastel identity palette and accessories at runtime, replace the T-pose with a relaxed standing pose, and add deterministic blink/body motion while preserving real state, expression and audio-derived viseme control.
non_goals: Do not claim the runtime customization is a commissioned, owner-final or production-exclusive VRM asset.
Do not sexualize or add revealing clothing to the childlike/chibi character design.
Do not download a model, texture, motion or other runtime asset from the network.
Do not add a new package, change backend business logic or open M08.
Do not run the deferred frozen OBS benchmark or eight-hour soak in this iteration.
contracts: The desktop CSP may allow blob only for img-src and connect-src so Three.js ImageBitmapLoader can decode GLB-embedded textures; script, HTTP/WebSocket connect, object, frame and navigation boundaries remain unchanged.
The fixed bundled VRM path, upstream file bytes and embedded license metadata remain unchanged.
Hina palette, pose, blink and accessory geometry are repository-original runtime presentation code with no external asset or network requirement.
The base pose lowers both upper arms away from the VRM bind/T-pose, keeps clothing modest and uses bounded state motion.
Real backend state, expression and audio-spectral viseme values remain the only product animation inputs; decorative idle motion does not create fake conversation or speech.
Unknown expression still falls back to neutral and all generated rotation, blink and material values remain finite and bounded.
Renderer failure still falls back to the code-native SVG and can retry only the fixed local VRM.
allowed_dependencies: Existing Three.js, @pixiv/three-vrm, Vue and Electron dependencies.
Existing fixed VRM1_Constraint_Twist_Sample asset and its embedded textures.
Repository-original JavaScript profile data and Three.js primitive geometry.
No new package, binary asset, model, texture or network dependency.
acceptance_criteria: A unit test proves img-src permits local/data/blob images and connect-src permits only self/blob for ImageBitmap decoding, with no HTTP/WebSocket source introduced.
Pure Node tests cover all six avatar states, finite bounded pose output, visibly lowered symmetric upper arms, deterministic blink and complete material palette mappings.
The desktop reports the named Hina presentation and a non-zero loaded embedded texture count.
The real Electron capture visibly contains colored hair, skin, eyes and clothing instead of a white untextured model.
The avatar starts in a relaxed standing pose, not a horizontal-arm T-pose, and state motion remains subtle.
The Hina presentation adds original pastel accessories and keeps the generic upstream VRM provenance visible in the UI.
Desktop typecheck, unit/security tests and one real Electron smoke pass.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
